channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Tavers: The Meow Wolf RPG Expands Minds On Kickstarter
item.content:encoded:
The psychedelic Tavers: The Meow Wolf RPG has gone live on Kickstarter. The experimental TTRPG, developed alongside the Meow Wolf immersive art company, Exalted Funeral, and Genuine Entertainment has already reached its initial funding goal.

According to its campaign page, Tavers: The Meow Wolf RPG is a perception-altering tabletop RPG based on the aesthetics and concepts of the Meow Wolf company's past immersive art displays. The game is designed by Exalted Funeral (Monty Python RPG) and Genuine Entertainment (The Walking Dead RPG).

In the game, players take on the role of Tavers. Tavers are a gifted group of people that can sense and access the Source, a raw creative energy that flows through everything. They can also sense the Tave, a psychic network of portals that connects to various worlds beyond. With these gifts, Tavers can explore bizarre, exotic places, and even bend reality by manifesting impossible ideas.

If you're an RPG veteran or newcomer, Tavers: The Meow Wolf RPG is designed to be light on complexity. The game supports an open storytelling style and lightweight rules systems. In addition, it emphasizes worldbuilding, character building, and story building without anyone having to parse pages of lore.

Those game mechanics include a light 2d8-based system, skills fueled by character backstory, goal oriented character progression, and a tactile, Tetris-esque inventory management system called Mem. For fans of Meow Wolf's exhibits, the game will include locations inspired by them, such as the Glowquarium, Convergence Station, and The Forked Earth.

The different pledge tiers for Tavers: The Meow Wolf RPG are as follows:

  • Techstreamer's Delight for $25: A PDF copy of the core rulebook, digital game materials, a soundtrack, and all digital stretch goals
  • Hi-Volume Volume for $45: A physical copy of the core rulebook, digital game materials, and all digital and physical stretch goals
  • The Quantum Parabox for $65: The Quantum Parabox set, which contains the core rulebook, 3 custom d8s, sets of tokens, character sheets, itemtronical sheets, sticker art sheets, and a Tavers reference chart. Pledge also includes digital soundtrack and all stretch goals
  • Holo Infoilstructure Volume for $75: A Kickstarter-exclusive physical copy of the book, digital soundtrack, and all stretch goals
  • Portal Party! for $95: The Quantum Parabox, a Mem Maker Kit, a Tavers Toolkit Refill Pack, digital soundtrack, and all stretch goals
  • Psychic Panoramic for $195: The Quantum Parabox, Limited Edition physical core rulebook, Mem Maker Kit, Tavers Toolkit Refill Pack, a soundtrack LP and digital files, a Tavers logo patch, and all stretch goals

At the time of writing, Tavers: The Meow Wolf RPG has raised $71,971, meeting its initial funding goal of $50,000. It has also unlocked its first stretch goal: an acetate dust jacket for the core rulebook. Additional stretch goals include more book content and painted page edges.

The campaign ends on April 3, 2026. Materials are expected to ship on December 2026.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler

    Additional topics:

  • Announcement

  • RPG



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/tavers-meow-wolf-rpg-expands-minds-on-kickstarter
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Finally Confirmed by Ubisoft
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has officially confirmed that one of the gaming industry's worst-kept secrets, Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced, exists, surprising perhaps the three or four people in the world who hadn't already heard the project was underway.

In a blog post outlining the near future of the Assassin's Creed series, the franchise's new leadership team shares a piece of artwork from Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced alongside the message that "some whispers have a little more wind in their sails" than other "speculation".

This is the first official confirmation of a game that's been part of the industry rumor mill for quite a while now, not to mention one that was confirmed by a PEGI rating back in December. Sorry, Ubisoft; I think you're a little behind the curve on this one.

Elsewhere in the post, the team says that support for Assassin's Creed Shadows will be winding down following its next major update, a decision that chimes with Ubisoft revealing that there would be no second paid expansion for the game back in November.

Meanwhile, development on the as-yet-unannounced (sort of) Assassin's Creed Codename Hexe continues, and Ubisoft says it will be a "unique, darker, narrative-driven Assassin's Creed experience" developed by a "veteran team" at Ubisoft Montreal.

Work also continues on Codename Invictus, a PvP Assassin's Creed game that's being worked on by a team of For Honor veterans (also at the Montreal location).

Various other Assassin's Creed projects are also still in the works, including Jade, as well as the reintroduction of co-op to the series in some form, although we don't know exactly how just yet.

That's not all, though. If you've been looking for a reason to revisit 2014's maligned Assassin's Creed Unity, you'll be pleased to know a 60fps patch for the game is coming to PS5 and Xbox Series X|S tomorrow!

We still don't know when the Assassin's Creed Black Flag remake (or remaster, or whatever it turns out to be) will arrive, nor do we know what form Codename Hexe will take. Stay tuned for more on this and all other things Assassin's Creed.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/assassins-creed-black-flag-resynced-finally-confirmed
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
